reasoning: The 4-week moving average of insured unemployment claims (CC4WSA) has shown a recent steep upward trend over the last 4 weeks (rising from 1922k to 1947.5k). The value on Oct 25 (the likely anchor for Oct 26) is near the upper bound of the recent 5-month range (peaking around 1951k in August). Short-term momentum strongly suggests an increase over the next week to Nov 2, leading to a moderately high probability. For Nov 25 and Jan 2026, uncertainty increases and the likelihood of mean-reversion pulling the average below the relatively high 1947.5k mark pushes the probability closer to 0.5 and slightly below.
